title: Read cap enforced per-renderer (structureForRead/{read}) not at the output boundary — the {json} channel bypasses it on both CLI --json and in-pi Pi-tool surfaces
description: Root cause surfaced by the TASK-012 adversarial re-audit. The 50KB read cap + FGAP-089 fail-closed REFUSAL lives inside structureForRead (used only by OpResult {read}); the {json} channel returns a raw value with no cap. So a {json} op embedding substrate content emits it unbounded on BOTH surfaces: the CLI --json envelope (cli.ts:492, output=r.json) and the in-pi Pi-tool tool-result text (ops-registry.ts:1927, renderOpResultText -> JSON.stringify(r.json) into the agent context — the exact context blowup the cap exists to prevent). Two confirmed unbounded {json} emitters: resolve-item-by-id (full ItemLocation incl. item body) and promote-item (PromotionResult embeds an item via ResolvedRef.loc). The one-at-a-time audit discovery (read-block, then resolve-item-by-id, then promote-item) is the signature of the missing invariant. Same gap-class the parity-check gate already closes for op-coverage/ctx-forwarding.

evidence.reference: registerAll Pi-tool text = renderOpResultText -> JSON.stringify(r.json) uncapped into agent context
resolve-item-by-id returns { json: full ItemLocation } — unbounded content emitter
promote-item returns { json: PromotionResult } embedding an item via ResolvedRef.loc
structureForRead applies truncateHead; only {read} ops route through it — the cap is a renderer property, not a boundary invariant

impact: Any op emitting substrate content via {json} leaks it unbounded on both the CLI --json surface and the in-pi agent context, defeating the FGAP-089 fail-closed read cap. Per-op patching is whack-a-mole; the next {json} content-emitter (existing or future) re-opens it.
proposed_resolution: CLOSED at 75f2611 (TASK-013), per DEC-0009. The read cap is now enforced at the output boundary for every channel: a runtime boundary cap (boundedJsonOutput + cap-applying renderOpResultText) fail-closes any over-cap {json}/prose on all three emit sites (CLI --json, CLI text, in-pi Pi-tool); resolve-item-by-id + promote-item route through {read} for graceful degradation; a parity-check gate (checkJsonContentCap) FAILS an op directly returning {json} of a content-reading call, with the runtime cap as the backstop for helper-chain misses. Adversarial re-audit confirmed the recurrence ended — no read/query op ships unbounded data on either surface. Pre-existing truncateHead pretty-vs-compact/line-cap over-refusal tracked as FGAP-016 (non-leak).
